subject: Circuit installation/setup UX from a first-time end user landing on the GitHub repo wanting to try it in Claude Code
recommendation: The documented happy path is a 4-step flow in README.md L17-30: (1) `/plugin marketplace add petekp/circuit`, (2) `/plugin install circuit@petekp`, (3) `/reload-plugins`, (4) `/circuit:run {{task}}`. An optional 5th step is dropping `~/.claude/circuit.config.yaml` from `circuit.config.example.yaml` (README L39-43). Confirmed facts: the marketplace-install path is stated up front and `/reload-plugins` is explicitly noted to obviate a session restart (README L25); `/circuit:run` is the single front-door entry point. Confirmed friction (from prior survey at .circuit/circuit-runs/ux-issues-opportunities/phases/survey-install-setup/reports/install-setup-findings.md): (a) Node 20+ is listed as a prerequisite but no version check is enforced, so Node 18 users only fail at runtime (High confidence); (b) outdated 'relay templates' terminology at README L236 conflicts with current 'prompt contracts' / surface-manifest.json vocabulary (High); (c) config setup is buried 4 paragraphs deep and is silently optional, so first runs may use suboptimal defaults (Medium); (d) 'start a new Claude Code session' phrasing is ambiguous against `/reload-plugins` (Medium); (e) Troubleshooting cites a `reap-legacy-handoffs.sh` path that mismatches actual location (Low). Assumptions still requiring proof: that the marketplace handle `petekp/circuit` resolves cleanly in current Claude Code plugin UX, and that `/reload-plugins` reliably surfaces Circuit slash commands without a fresh session — neither was exercised end-to-end in this run. Trustworthiness proof needed: a clean-machine walkthrough running the four commands verbatim and capturing where a new user would first stall (likely the Node prereq with no guardrail, or the silent config skip). Rubric: weight High-confidence findings as actionable now, Medium as worth fixing once verified by a real first-run trial, Low as polish.
success_condition_alignment: Names the inspected evidence (README.md L17-43 for the install flow, prior install-setup-findings.md survey for friction points), separates confirmed facts (the documented commands and the surveyed High-confidence findings) from assumptions (marketplace handle resolution and `/reload-plugins` end-to-end behavior were not exercised), and identifies the proof that would make the recommendation trustworthy (a clean-machine walkthrough capturing first-stall points).

supporting_aspects.contribution: Frames the user journey as the literal command sequence a GitHub visitor would copy from the README, bounding the answer to the install + first-run window rather than deep workflow usage.
Cites the README install section and a prior survey report as the inspected sources, and calls out the missing proof: an actual clean-machine first-run trial.

Separates confirmed friction (Node version unenforced, outdated terminology, buried config guidance, ambiguous 'new session' wording, mispathed troubleshooting script) from assumptions (marketplace handle resolution, `/reload-plugins` behavior end-to-end), preserving uncertainty where direct run evidence is absent.
